note: PLANNED — no database, Supabase project, migrations, or connection exists in the TDS repo today (runtime deps are only react + react-dom). This is an EXEMPLARY, non-authoritative entity-relationship model for the FUTURE design-system platform backend. Target: Supabase Postgres 15+ with Auth + RLS + Storage + Edge Functions + Realtime, engineered to scale beyond 10,000,000 users. When the backend is built, this file becomes DATA-DERIVED and is regenerated by the PLANNED `npm run erd:build`; until then it illustrates the standard defined in docs/ai-os/14_DATABASE_GUIDE.md.

database.authOwnership: Supabase Auth owns `auth.users` (credentials, sessions, password hashes). Application profile rows live in public.users and reference auth.users(id) 1:1. NEVER store secrets/credentials/token values in public tables (Rule 13).

migrations.discipline: forward-only, append-only, idempotent-on-reset; never edit an applied migration; destructive changes follow expand -> migrate -> contract

designLock.statement: The database is NEVER a source of design truth. Design decisions (color, spacing, typography, variants, component structure) live ONLY in Storybook + `*.meta.ts` + `src/tokens/*`. The `design_systems`, `components`, and `tokens` tables below are NON-AUTHORITATIVE projections/caches of that source, kept for platform features (search, catalog, drift-detection, release history). On any disagreement the source wins and these rows are regenerated (Source-of-Truth Hierarchy, docs/ai-os/00_MASTER_RULES.md).

conventions.naming: tables plural snake_case; columns singular snake_case; enums singular; booleans read as predicates (is_*, has_*).
conventions.primaryKey: id uuid primary key default gen_random_uuid() on every table (no sequential integer PKs across a tenant boundary).
canonicalColumns: created_at timestamptz not null default now()
updated_at timestamptz not null default now() (maintained by set_updated_at() trigger)
deleted_at timestamptz (soft-delete; filtered `deleted_at is null`; never hard-delete tenant data on the user path)
conventions.tenancyKey: org_id uuid not null references organizations(id) on every multi-tenant table — the single dimension RLS and partitioning key on.
conventions.foreignKeys: explicit `references … (id)` with a deliberate `on delete` action; every FK and every tenancy key is indexed (Postgres does not auto-index FK columns).
conventions.types: prefer timestamptz over timestamp, text + check over varchar(n), numeric for money, jsonb for schemaless attributes, native enum for closed sets.
conventions.rlsPosture: RLS enabled on every table in public with explicit policies; a table with RLS enabled and no policy denies all access (deny-by-default).

sharedFunctions.name: public.set_updated_at()
sharedFunctions.purpose: Set new.updated_at := now() before every UPDATE. Attached as a BEFORE UPDATE FOR EACH ROW trigger on every mutable table.

sharedFunctions.name: public.is_org_member(target_org uuid)
sharedFunctions.purpose: Return true when auth.uid() has a row in org_members for target_org. Used inside RLS `using`/`with check` predicates to keep policies terse. SECURITY DEFINER, search_path pinned.

sharedFunctions.name: public.has_org_role(target_org uuid, min_role org_member_role)
sharedFunctions.purpose: Return true when auth.uid()'s role in target_org is at least min_role (owner > admin > member > viewer). Backs write policies.

enums.name: component_tier
enums.note: Mirrors the repo's 24 atoms / 27 molecules / 9 organisms tiering.

enums.name: token_collection
enums.note: Mirrors the 3 Figma Variable collections: Primitives -> Semantic -> Theme.

entities.name: organizations
entities.description: Tenancy root. Every multi-tenant row keys back here via org_id.

columns.name: slug
columns.description: URL-safe org handle.

rls.note: Org creation is handled by a SECURITY DEFINER RPC that also inserts the creator as an 'owner' org_member; there is no open INSERT policy.

entities.name: users
entities.description: Application profile, 1:1 with auth.users. Credentials/sessions are owned by Supabase Auth, never stored here.

columns.name: id
columns.description: Same id as the Supabase Auth user.

columns.name: email
columns.description: Mirror of auth email for display/search; not a credential.

policies.name: users_select_co_member
policies.using: exists (select 1 from public.org_members a join public.org_members b on a.org_id = b.org_id where a.user_id = auth.uid() and b.user_id = users.id)
policies.note: See profiles of users who share an org.

entities.name: org_members
entities.description: Membership join table: which user belongs to which org and with what role. The RLS spine — most policies resolve through this table.

entities.name: projects
entities.description: A workspace within an org that groups one or more registered design systems.

indexes.name: projects_org_status_created_idx
indexes.note: Hot path: list active projects in an org, newest first.

entities.name: design_systems
entities.description: Registry pointer to a design system (e.g. TDS) within a project. NON-AUTHORITATIVE: the real design source lives in the linked Storybook + repo (`*.meta.ts` + `src/tokens/*`). This row holds addressing + cached metadata only.

columns.name: key
columns.description: Stable slug, e.g. 'tds'.

columns.name: source_repo_url
columns.description: Git repo that is the actual source of truth.

columns.name: storybook_url
columns.description: Deployed Storybook — the design source of truth surface.

columns.name: figma_file_key
columns.description: Figma file the plugin reproduces into (a reproduction, never a source).

columns.name: latest_release_id
columns.description: Denormalized pointer; nullable to break the circular FK on insert.

columns.name: component_count
columns.description: Cached count; source of truth is metas.ts / docs/COMPONENTS.md.

entities.name: components
entities.description: Catalog/index rows mirroring the component metas (src/components/metas.ts) and docs/COMPONENTS.md. NON-AUTHORITATIVE cache for search/browse/drift-detection; never a source of design truth (Design Lock).

columns.name: name
columns.description: e.g. 'Button', 'Card', 'Dropdown'.

columns.name: tier
columns.description: atom | molecule | organism.

columns.name: variant_axes
columns.description: Mirror of meta variant axes (type/variant/tone/size/shape). Descriptive cache only.

columns.name: meta_hash
columns.description: Hash of the source *.meta.ts to detect drift between this cache and the source.

policies.name: components_write_admin
policies.note: In practice written by the PLANNED sync/importer service (service role), not end users.

entities.name: tokens
entities.description: Index rows mirroring src/tokens/generated (figma.tokens.json). NON-AUTHORITATIVE cache; the token source (src/tokens/*) wins on any disagreement.

columns.name: token_id
columns.description: Dot-notation id, e.g. 'color.brand.solid', 'space.4'.

columns.name: collection
columns.description: primitive | semantic | theme.

columns.name: css_var
columns.description: e.g. '--tds-color-brand-solid'.

columns.name: figma_name
columns.description: Slash-notation, e.g. 'color/brand/solid'.

columns.name: alias_of
columns.description: For Semantic/Theme aliases that point into Primitives (VARIABLE_ALIAS).

policies.name: tokens_write_admin
policies.note: Written by the PLANNED sync/importer (service role) from src/tokens/generated.

entities.name: releases
entities.description: Immutable, versioned snapshot of a design system (semver). Captures the generated manifest bundle at publish time.

columns.name: version
columns.description: Semver, e.g. '0.1.0'.

columns.name: manifest_url
columns.description: Storage URL of the snapshotted figma/tds.plugin.json + src/generated/design-system.manifest.json.

indexes.name: releases_one_current_idx
indexes.note: At most one current release per design system.

policies.name: releases_update_admin
policies.note: Published rows are treated as immutable except toggling is_current.

entities.name: figma_syncs
entities.description: Audit of one-way Storybook -> Figma reproduction runs (the plugin rebuilding the system from the bundle). Records status and coverage; sync never flows Figma -> source.

entities.name: audit_log
entities.description: Append-only activity/audit trail. Range-partitioned by month at scale; retention via partition detach. Never updated or deleted on the user path.
entities.primaryKeyNote: Composite because the range partition key (created_at) MUST be part of the primary key.

columns.name: entity_type
columns.description: e.g. 'projects', 'releases', 'components'.

policies.name: audit_log_select_admin
policies.note: Only org admins/owners read the audit trail.

policies.name: audit_log_insert_member
policies.note: Typically written by SECURITY DEFINER triggers / the service role; no UPDATE or DELETE policy (append-only).

relationships.note: Profile mirrors the Supabase Auth user.
Nullable denormalized pointer; circular with releases.design_system_id — insert release first or null then backfill.

rlsModel.posture: deny-by-default; RLS enabled on every public table with explicit policies keyed on auth.uid().
rlsModel.tenancy: Access is granted through org_members: a user sees an org's rows only if they hold a membership row (public.is_org_member(org_id)).
rlsModel.roles: org_member_role (owner > admin > member > viewer) gates writes via public.has_org_role(org_id, min_role).
rlsModel.ownership: Row-owner predicates (owner_id = auth.uid(), id = auth.uid()) refine access on top of tenancy.
rlsModel.serviceRole: Sync/importer/audit writers run as the Supabase service role, which bypasses RLS; those paths carry their own authorization checks in the PLANNED Node/API layer.
rlsModel.denyByDefault: A table with RLS enabled and no matching policy returns zero rows / rejects the write — the intended default.

scaling.connectionPooling: All app traffic through Supavisor/PgBouncer in transaction mode; direct connections reserved for migrations.
scaling.readReplicas: Route read-heavy traffic to Postgres read replicas; writes hit the primary (chosen by read/write intent in the API layer).
scaling.partitioning: Range-partition append-heavy tables (audit_log, and future notifications/events) by created_at monthly; automate partition creation + retention/detach.
scaling.sharding: Horizontal ceiling: shard by org_id across databases — every tenant table already carries org_id (Rule 6), so the routing key exists from day one.
scaling.indexing: Index every FK + tenancy key; add composite/partial/covering/GIN/BRIN indexes for measured hot paths only (no speculative indexes).

generationPlan.script: npm run erd:build (PLANNED — scripts/build-ai-manifests.ts or a dedicated erd builder)
generationPlan.source: The applied migration history under supabase/migrations/ (introspected schema) — NOT hand-authored once the backend exists.
generationPlan.syncRule: Regenerate and commit this file in the same change as any schema migration; a diff means the manifest was not regenerated (docs/ai-os/14_DATABASE_GUIDE.md Rule 12).

validation.notes: When promoted from SEED to DATA-DERIVED, the PLANNED gates in 14_DATABASE_GUIDE.md §7 apply: naming lint, RLS-enabled gate, FK-index gate, migration idempotency, ERD-in-sync check, type-sync check, policy smoke tests, schema-drift check.
